channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: FFXIV's Patch 7.50 Has Arrived, But There's Plenty More To Come In This Patch Series Throughout 2026
item.content:encoded:
Today starts the beginning of the end of the Dawntrail saga, as Final Fantasy 14 has launched its Patch 7.50, titled “Trail to the Heavens.” This specific update today features major story updates, an Alliance Raid, huge quality of life changes and more anticipated additions. 

While a lot of content arrives today with this patch, it’s only the start of what’s to come, as this patch is the first of several to come in a “patch series” that’ll span much of 2026. 

What's out today in FFXIV Patch 7.50? 

For this first update in the series, many players are eagerly awaiting the Main Scenario Quests, which will begin to bring them towards the plot of the newly-announced Evercold expansion. As part of this, there’s a fresh dungeon in Garlemald called the Clyteum, as well as a new trial, The Unmaking, featuring a refreshed version of a secret boss from Final Fantasy 5. 

The finale of the Alliance Raids themed to fellow franchise MMORPG Final Fantasy 11 arrives. Titled “Windurst: The Third Walk,” the fan-favorite Black Mage Shantotto is heavily featured in the previews. PvP players will get to hop onto the new Archeia Harmonias map, which features neat new mechanics that can stop the crystal from moving, healing spots, and bridges that need to be activated to move the crystal.  

There are also many of the quality of life changes that the team promised during the Live Letters. Dyes are combined mostly into three specific dyeables, many job-specific and dungeon gear sets are placeable in the armourie, and now, you can do tasks between crafting without closing the interface, which means you can drink a potion without awkwardly standing up and sitting back down immediately. 

Plus, some content has been tweaked. In Deep Dungeons, such as Palace of the Dead, party KO counts won’t start racking up until certain floors—100 for PotD, and 30 for all others. Also, for Cloud of Darkness (Chaotic), the 48-man raid, it can be taken on unsynced, but minimum item level runs will guarantee all of the much-desired drops in the chests. Ocean fishing also gets a neat new Thavnair map. 

Last but certainly not least, the much-acclaimed Shadowbringers expansion is now looped into the free trial.

What’s coming in the rest of FFXIV's 7.5X patch series? 

Some big additions are forthcoming in FFXIV’s 7.5X patch series. Generally, as this is the end-of-expansion patch, the team likes to spread things out a little bit more. 

Coming in about five weeks on June 5 is the 7.51 update, which brings the last planet of Cosmic Exploration, the final waves of Dawntrail’s Custom Deliveries, and the Dancing Mad Ultimate featuring Kefka. 

Then, on July 28—right after the Berlin FanFest—we’ll get the 7.55 update that rounds out the non-MSQ content with the finales for Hildibrand and Occult Crescent. 

Finally, 7.56 arrives on September 8, as the last of the main patches brings the much-awaited Beastmaster job, shown off during the last Live Letter, and the end of Dawntrail's Main Scenario Quests, rounding out the story and leading us into FFXIV's upcoming Evercold expansion’s storyline. 

Victoria Rose

Victoria has professionally written about games since 2016, including small former tenures with Polygon and Fanbyte. She mostly spends time in FFXIV, head-deep in roleplay campaigns or stubbornly playing Black Mage through high-end raids. Former obsessions include Dota 2 and The Secret World (also mostly roleplaying). Come visit their estate: Diabolos (Crystal DC), Goblet, Ward 4, Plot 28.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/ffxivs-patch-750-has-arrived-but-theres-plenty-more-to-come-in-this-patch-series-throughout-2026-2000137921
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Preview the Night's Den, a Special House Earned in The Elder Scrolls Online's Night Market Starting Tomorrow
item.content:encoded:
Tomorrow, the Night Market opens up for a limited time in The Elder Scrolls Online. With Season Zero in full swing and more still to discover, the ESO team is previewing one of the biggest rewards the first Night Market will offer: A new house with several special features.

The Night’s Den house is a reward that you earn by completing the introductory quest (“Those Who Would Rule” ) for the Night Market, so it's something you'll earn pretty quickly.  This is good, because it’s not just a house. Your participation in the Night Market will reflect in the house itself.

First, the home is manor-sized. By pledging to a faction and earning 1,000 Favor, you'll unlock a banner with details that indicate which faction has your loyalty. There’s more though. “It gains additional options over future Night Market appearances if you choose to support different factions”. Hmm.

As you progress through the event zone, find Night Market relic fragments in each district to unlock relics. When you Unlock a relic, you unlock a collectible Furnishing that will be used to display that Relic and a container of additional furnishings all patterned after a Night Market District. When you put that Relic on its special display furnishing you unlock another wing of the house. 

That's not the only way that things grow, because you can also unlock a special vault that will allow access to personal and guild bank storage from inside. As you progress and gain more wealth, one of the rooms in the house will even shift with gold on display with 10 available levels depending on your own wealth in the bank. 

Scrooge McDuck style gold diving doesn't seem to be an option. But maybe in the future.

The Night Market will open up tomorrow, and run through June 17th before it disappears until a future season.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/preview-the-nights-den-a-special-house-earned-in-the-elder-scrolls-onlines-night-market-starting-tomorrow-2000137919
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Empires of EVE Author Is Kickstarting A New MMO Book Project Called The Archive: Epics of Virtual History
item.content:encoded:
Empires of EVE author Andrew Groen is kickstarting a new book, this time expanding outside of New Eden to catalog the stories of many different MMO virtual worlds. 

Groen's latest endeavor is called The Archive: Epics of Virtual History, and aims to preserve the history of some of the genre's greatest moments. Drawing on his extensive history cataloging the growing narrative of one of the most complex MMOs on the market, EVE Online, Groen's The Archive brings that storytelling to the broader world of MMOs. 

The book aims to document the history of virtual worlds, from EVE Online to EverQuest, Final Fantasy XIV, World of Warcraft and more. 

Via the Kickstarter page:

"Inspired by ancient history books like the Sagas of Icelanders, The Archive: Epics of Virtual History is an artifact dedicated to preserving gaming's cultural history through the ages. 

In some cases the worlds of the online realm are already disappearing, but The Archive preserves their memory by capturing the stories that defined their history."

Groen states that the volume is actually already complete and is "nearly ready to go to print." He teases some of the history we will see in this volume of The Archive, including The First Scarab Lord from World of Warcraft, The Siege of Caiger Mall from Urban Dead, The Return of the Hopeslayer from Asheron's Call, and more. 

Right now Groen is asking for a Kickstarter goal of $20K, but with stretch goals the volume could see additional content added before it goes to print, including improved artwork and an additional chapter which will include an interview with Final Fantasy XIV's Naoki Yoshida.

You can back the project here. As of the time of this writing, the funding amount is nearing the $10K mark, just a few hours after going live. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/empires-of-eve-author-is-kickstarting-a-new-mmo-book-project-called-the-archive-epics-of-virtual-history-2000137920
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Guild Wars Reforged Arrives On Mobile Platforms This Summer
item.content:encoded:
ArenaNet and 2Weeks have announced that a mobile version of Guild Wars Reforged, the revamp of the original Guild Wars, will be coming to Android and iOS this summer. 

Released back in December, Guild Wars Reforged is the modern, active edition of the “classic” original GW MMORPG run by a studio called 2Weeks that includes some former ArenaNet staffers. While it mostly replicates the original experience, the new version features a sweep of quality of life features, including controller support, user interface revamps, and some minor visual upgrades. 

As explained by game director Stephen Clarke-Willson in a press release, building and optimizing Forge for the Steam Deck helped to inform the user interface capacities of the upcoming mobile version. 

Reforged’s mobile version will include mobile-specific optimized user interfaces and controls. It’ll be a free version supported by ads, though buying any of the expansions will remove the ads. Thankfully, there’ll also be cross-play, cross-save and cross-progression. 

Those who have bought an expansion on PC will also be able to sync it up to the mobile version, thus disabling ads. Otherwise, the Eye of the North expansion and Bonus Mission Pack will be up for grabs for, respectively, $9.99 (£8.99) and $5.99 (£5.49).

There’s no exact date for the release yet, but pre-registration is already up on the Google Play Store, with the Apple App Store’s page coming soon.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/guild-wars-reforged-arrives-on-mobile-platforms-this-summer-2000137918
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Children's Week is Back in World of Warcraft, So Adventure with Azeroth's Orphans and Earn New Stuff
item.content:encoded:
Champion the orphaned when you give back to the children of Azeroth during Children's Week—take an orphan under your wing and show them what the hero's life is like!

When: April 27–May 4

Children's Week in WoW: Midnight

What's New in 2026

  • Children's Week comes to Midnight's reimagined Silvermoon City in Quel'Thalas!
  • New Toy: Nap Mat
  • A new Children's Week vendor is standing near the Holiday Enthusiast, between the Auction House and the Trading Post, in the reimagined Silvermoon City of Midnight.

Orphans from All Over Azeroth

Visit Orphan Matron Nightingale in Stormwind, Orphan Matron Battlewail in Orgrimmar, Orphan Matron Mercy in Shattrath, Orphan Matron Aria in Dalaran, Caretake Padae in Dazar'alor, or Orphan Matron Westerson in Boralus, or Matron in Training Ullna in Khaz Algar, and make a child's dream come true!

There are a handful of orphans, eager for adventure, to choose from:

  • Horde Orphans: Azala, Grunth, Salandria
    • Maybe little Salandria would like a visit to the Sunwell–hint,hint.
  • Alliance Orphans: Randis, Dornaa, and Liam
  • Neutral Orphans: Roo (Eventide and Dalaran), Kekek (Eventide and Dalaran), Destien (Azj-Kahet, Dornogal, and Undermine), Kitzy (Azj-Kahet, Dornogal, and Hallowfall), Skibbles (Dornogal), and Threadis (Azj-Kahet, Dornogal, and Hallowfall).

Currency and Rewards

Players can select the Well-loved Figurine as a reward for completing a Children's Week questline. The Well-loved Figurine is Warbound, takes up a bag slot, is only redeemable during Children's Week, and disappears once the holiday event concludes.

Completing select Children's Week activities with your little protégé in tow also helps to fill up your bar in the Traveler's Log.

Exchange the Well-loved Figurine for any of the Children's Week pets or transmog items from vendors near the Orphan Matrons in Dornogal (Jepetto Joybuzz, Stormwind (Brundia Braidhammer), and Orgrimmar (Leial Knitterton).

Transmog Sets

Pets

Showing your orphaned child around can benefit you and the child. Once you've completed the various quests associated with this event, you'll have a choice of several pets to add to your Battle Pet collection, and this is the only Week of the year when you can obtain pets:

  • Khaz Algar: Helpful Workshop Bot, Argos, and Goggles
  • Boralus and Zuldazar: Mr. Crabs, Beakbert, Froglet, and Scaley
  • Dalaran (Northrend): Curious Oracle Hatchling, Curious Wolvar Pup
  • Shattrath: Egbert, Peanut, Willy, Legs
  • Orgrimmar and Stormwind: Whiskers the Rat, Speedy, Scooter the Snail, Mr. Wiggles

If you've already collected all these pets over the years, you'll get the Pet Care Package, which provides a variety of items, including pug costumes, a Magical Pet Biscuit, and a Flawless Battle-Stone.

Achievements

Meta-Achievement: For the ChildrenComplete the following Children’s Week achievements listed below, and you’ll earn the title reward of Matron or Patron: Home Alone; Daily Chores; Bad Example; Aw, Isn’t It Cute?; and Hail to the King, Baby.

Home Alone (Part of the Meta-Achievement)Use your Hearthstone while your orphan is with you.

Daily Chores (Part of the Meta-Achievement)Complete five daily quests with your orphan out.

Bad Example (Part of the Meta-Achievement)Eat the sweets listed below while your orphan is watching.

  • Tigule’s Strawberry Ice Cream, Tasty Cupcake, Red Velvet Cupcake, Delicious Chocolate Cake, Lovely Cake Slice, Dalaran Brownie, Dalaran Doughnut

Aw, Isn’t It Cute? (Part of the Meta-Achievement) Obtain one of the Children’s Week reward pets through questing.

Hail to the King, Baby (Part of the Meta-Achievement)Defeat King Ymiron in Utgarde Pinnacle with your orphan out.

Veteran Nanny Acquire Egbert’s Egg, Sleepy Willy, and Elekk Training Collar on one character.

  • These are the items that unlock the battle pets Egbert, Willy, and Peanut.

WoW Classic

The WoW Classic version of Children's Week is also available for the giving souls of the Horde and the Alliance to help make orphan dreams come true. Talk to an Orphan Matron to pick up a pal you can guide around Azeroth.

When: April 27–May 4

Players level 10 and above can visit Orphan Matron Nightingale in Stormwind (Alliacne), Orphan Matron Battlewail in Orgrimmar (Horde), Orphan Matron Mercy in Shattrath (Neutral), or Orphan Matron Aria in Dalaran (Neutral) and make a child's dream come true.

Champions of the Horde can adopt Orc and Blood Elf orphans, while the heroes of the Alliance can adopt Human and Draenei orphans. Both factions can adopt either of the Oracles or the Frenzyheart Tribe orphans.

Upon completing the requested tasks from a chosen orphan, players have their choice of a cute, non-combat pet! These pets are account-wide, so feel free to share them with your alts.

Have fun traveling around the world with your little buddy by your side! For tips and tricks on making the most of the holiday, head over to  Wowhead's Holiday Guide or Wowhead's Classic Holiday Guide.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/childrens-week-is-back-in-world-of-warcraft-so-adventure-with-azeroths-orphans-and-earn-new-stuff-2000137917
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
